notes: Smallest possible round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the readyâ†’workingâ†’ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE aider driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh: `aider --no-auto-commits --yes-always --no-gitignore` in a tmux REPL inside a fresh per-run git cwd, one send + wait_turn + 8s trailing sleep). aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the session by proc-<PID> rather than a transcript UUID â€” there is no separate rollout session phase, the whole arc stays on the one proc-<PID> row. The daemon observes that session born ready on the first scanner tick, the working flip when the `#### ` user-prompt line lands, the workingâ†’ready settle when aider's turn-end is inferred (the `> Tokens:` line closes the model call and IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after ~1.5s of quiescence, clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll), then process_exited at the single tmux kill-session teardown. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. WHY interactive, not headless `aider --message` (drift fix, same shape as the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall + pi cells): a headless --message run exits ~2.6s after the working flip â€” BEFORE the workingâ†’ready settle â€” so the daemon never observed the terminal ready edge and turn_end + teardown were previously known_failing (3/5). Keeping aider alive in the REPL across the turn + an 8s trailing sleep (one full ~1.5s idle-flush cycle plus the 5s working-refresh poll plus slack) lets the daemon observe the full readyâ†’workingâ†’ready settle, then one clean process_exited â€” all five phases now assert as passing. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by the aider parser unit test TestParser_BaselineHello_FullTurn.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Assistant reply completes â†’ back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless aider exits after the single turn â€” process_exited on the session
